description: ¬ß6.8 default= + reset(@cell) (RT): a cell with `default=` distinct from its init. [change, reset] drives query init-val‚Üítyped‚Üíreset-val; reset(@query) writes the default= expression ('reset-val'), NOT the init ('init-val') ‚Äî proving default= is used at reset time.
rationale: ¬ß6.8.1/¬ß6.8.2: when a cell carries `default=`, `reset(@cell)` evaluates the `default=` expression (at reset time) and writes it ‚Äî NOT the init expression. default='reset-val' differs from init='init-val', so a reset after a change lands 'reset-val', proving default= (not init re-eval) is used.
